title: Container Creation, Process Lifecycle, CLI State, and Cleanup · Delivery Case: Docker Create Run Start Boundary
summary: A support utility must run repeatedly with explicit inputs, observable exit behavior, bounded names, and cleanup that never deletes unrelated state. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker create run start boundary increment in deterministic container lifecycle runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish create, run, start, attach, exec, stop, kill, restart, pause, wait, remove, and auto-remove state transitions.
Capture client, server, API, context, platform, storage, security-option, and plugin versions before changing Docker state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern

Inspect identity and state, preserve evidence, then perform label-scoped cleanup. Competency probe: Distinguish create, run, start, attach, exec, stop, kill, restart, pause, wait, remove, and auto-remove state transitions. content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-containers-lifecycle-cli-5
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-containers-lifecycle-cli-5
    artifact: container-lifecycle.yaml
    decision: worked docker containers lifecycle cli 5
    scenario-variant: 4
    environment: ephemeral development host with constrained disk and memory
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      create: immutable-configured-container
      start: same-container-identity
      inspect: state-before-mutation
      cleanup: label-scoped-and-previewed
    verify: docker container inspect training-web --format json
    failure: Reject name-only cleanup, unbounded prune, or a claim that stop and remove preserve writable-layer state.
    repair: Use explicit IDs or labels, inspect before mutation, capture exit state, and remove only the scoped disposable resource.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
